evals.name: parallel feature worktree
evals.prompt: A user wants to start a risky feature while keeping the current branch untouched. Describe the worktree workflow.
evals.expected_output: The response uses git worktree for isolation, checks current status, chooses branch/path, creates worktree, and explains how to work/clean up safely.
assertions: The output says a git worktree is appropriate for isolated parallel feature work.
The output checks current git status or dirty state before creating the worktree.
The output chooses or asks for a branch name and worktree path.
The output creates or describes git worktree add workflow.
The output mentions cleanup/removal after the work is done.

evals.name: boundary simple branch switch
evals.prompt: A user says: switch from branch feature-a to feature-b. No parallel work is needed. Describe how the worktree skill should respond.
evals.expected_output: The response says a worktree is unnecessary for simple branch switching and routes to normal git checkout/switch workflow after status check.
assertions: The output says a git worktree is not needed for simple branch switching.
The output recommends normal git switch/checkout workflow instead.
The output still checks for uncommitted changes before switching.
The output avoids creating extra worktrees unnecessarily.
